title: pi-context substrate merge (+ cherry-pick) — structure-aware 3-way reconciliation of two divergent substrate versions
description: A defined merge operation over items, edges, config, and objects, keyed off the content-parent DAG: computes the merge base from lineage, 3-way-merges per item with field-kind dispatch (sequence fields via FEAT-003), set-merges edges, unions the object store, 3-way-merges config/schema, records multi-parent lineage, re-validates all invariants, and emits a typed conflict set. Cherry-pick is a scoped sub-case. Per DEC-0004.
acceptance_criteria: For base B and descendants X, Y the driver produces one merged state taking all non-conflicting changes from both, with a typed conflict set for the rest, and identical output regardless of which side is named 'ours'
The merge base is computed from the content_parent DAG, not supplied externally
Edges merge by set semantics; the object store merges by union; sequence fields merge via the convergent sequence field-type (FEAT-003)
The merged state passes the full invariant + lens-validator pass, or every failure appears in the conflict set
Merged items record content_parent referencing both source versions (multi-parent)
A single-item delta from one branch applies onto another (cherry-pick) through the same machinery
